step-1.description: Make both pane and sidebar scrollbars visibly thicker via fuller glyphs
criteria: The scrollbar thumb renders a fuller glyph than today for both the focused-pane and the unfocused/sidebar paths
The track no longer uses the hardcoded glyph literal; the track is visibly denser
Both the pane scrollback scrollbar and the sidebar list scrollbars (workspace list, agent panel) are visibly thicker
No geometry or hit-test change is required for the glyph swap; existing scrollbar drag works unchanged
Unit tests assert the thumb renders the fuller glyph and the track no longer emits the hardcoded literal for both pane and sidebar callers
just check passes

step-2.description: Add a right-aligned Alt-shortcut section to the bottom hint bar
criteria: The hint bar renders a right-aligned Alt-shortcut section sourcing key strings from active keybinds
Many-bindings show the Alt-modifier alternative, not the primary prefix form; entries with no Alt alternative after remap are dropped
Alt-shortcut entries are sanitized for control/bidi characters explicitly
Degrades full labels then short labels then drop Alt section then ellipsis; sections never overlap (left_used + right_width <= width enforced, including pad==0 and would-be-negative boundaries)
No input/dispatch change
Unit tests cover each FR4 tier as a distinct transition, Many-binding Alt resolution, drop-on-remap, and bidi/control sanitization
just check passes

step-3.description: Clear stale drag state on tab switch, workspace switch, and outer focus loss
criteria: Active drag state is cleared on tab switch, workspace switch, and outer-focus-lost
Existing tab-activation paths route through a single tab-switch chokepoint where the clear fires
press then move then (tab switch / workspace switch / outer-focus-lost) then move leaves drag cleared and changes no pane ratio or scroll offset
Unit tests cover each of the three clear triggers
just check passes

step-4.description: Widen the minimized sidebar rail into a button-like interactive surface with a prominent toggle
criteria: Rail width computed by a function returning 4..=7 cols, clamped to total width with a 4-col floor, wired through main view computation keeping the sidebar rect / debug assert consistent
Each row renders as a button-like cell (bg on active/selected/hover, centered icon, optional attention marker, no text), no collision at widths 4 and 7
Workspace-row click switches workspace; attention detail-row click focuses the pane; non-attention detail row does not steal focus
Rail-click switch/focus paths route through step-3's drag-clearing chokepoints
Render and hit-test consume the same shared collapsed-rail layout (ws_area, divider_y, detail_area, toggle)
Below the detail-area height floor, status-jump is inert while workspace rows stay switchable
Toggle rendered with accent treatment and hit area >=1 cell larger than its glyph; rect and hit-test match
Resize handle (design 3e) is out of scope; its NFR5 divider clause is not a gate here
Mobile/web path verified; restating the phone success measure is a recorded design-level decision, not a silent step outcome
Geometry/hit-rects computed compute-side, no state mutation in render(); assert_invariants_for_test passes after a rail-click
Unit tests cover boundary widths 4 and 7, height-boundary, workspace switch, attention vs non-attention jump, enlarged toggle, and in-bounds non-overlapping rects at the mobile threshold
just check passes

step-5.description: Replace tab compression/scroll with stateless centered-active batch overflow and zellij-style painting
criteria: With more tabs than fit, the active tab is always visible at full untruncated width; clickable left/right +N indicators appear per overflowing side and jump to the nearest hidden tab
Centered-active fill is stateless; prior scroll fields, their writers/readers, the compression tier, scroll-button widths, the chevron block, and related helpers are removed
tab_hit_areas stays a dense vec (len == tab count, hidden width 0); jump targets are tab indices, range-asserted
For every visible tab rect a position->index round-trip returns the same index; active tab rect width > 0; clicking each visible tab and each +N indicator activates the exact expected tab id
Tab width math uses Unicode display width; user-writable tab/workspace custom names are sanitized at the render chokepoint
Active tab renders accent bg / contrast fg; inactive/alternate tabs render the specified distinct coloring

Powerline separators render when ui.tabs.powerline is ON (default), fall back to alternating-background when OFF via an explicit painter param (no probing); OFF emits zero Powerline codepoints and only single-cell-width glyphs
+N indicator has a touch-adequate hit zone (NFR5)
ui.tabs.powerline (nested under ui, default ON) parses, is not flagged unknown, round-trips, and selects the separator style; roundtrip test covers OFF
Without mouse chrome the hidden count shows as a non-clickable marker; both branches use the centered fill
Drop-indicator coupling re-pointed at the new indicator rects with rewritten tests; reorder-drag drops at the correct index when an edge is clipped
Indicator-jump activation routes through the same tab-switch chokepoint that clears drag; press/move/indicator-jump/move test asserts drag cleared and no ratio/scroll change
Unit tests cover layout at N>width, identity round-trip with adversarial wide/combining/ZWJ names, non-mouse overflow branch, and separator paths; assert_invariants_for_test passes after a tab-jump
just check passes

step-6.description: Add attention badges to the spaces list, agent panel, and tab +N overflow indicators
criteria: Each indicator reports hidden count and hidden-attention count on the same single fill/scroll walk (no second O(n) rescan)
An attention badge renders only when >=1 hidden item is in attention, using the attention color, distinct from plain +N; no badge when none
Badge is clickable and jumps to the nearest hidden attention item (else nearest hidden) for spaces list, agent panel, and tab +N indicators
Each surface's jump target type is defined and range/identity-asserted; a jump landing on the wrong item fails its test
Spaces-list badge jump advances the scroll-aware window so the resolved target is no longer hidden after the click
Agent-panel badge-jump drag-clear behavior is explicitly decided (routes through a clearing path, or stated that focus-pane cannot strand a drag latch)
Spaces list and agent panel reuse the existing aggregate/attention/seen model, no new persisted state; predicate is Blocked or (Idle, seen=false)
Per-tab attention threaded into the tab-bar view without new persisted state
Sidebar rail bounded to a scroll-aware visible window (prerequisite for the spaces-list half)
Tab-bar attention work re-runs and preserves step-5's identity round-trip and assert_invariants_for_test
Geometry/hit-rects compute-side, no render mutation; badge is part of its indicator's clickable rect with a touch-adequate hit zone
Unit tests cover correct counts, badge-only-when-attention, click-to-nearest-attention (and nearest hidden when none), zero-hidden, and hidden-but-no-attention
just check passes
